questions.questionText: A rocket has a mass of 5000 kg. Its engines produce a thrust of 75 000 N. The weight of the rocket is 50 000 N. Calculate the acceleration of the rocket at launch.

steps.description: Recalls F = ma and rearranges to a = F/m

commonErrors: Using 75 000 N as the resultant force without subtracting weight
Adding thrust and weight instead of subtracting

questions.questionText: What is the weight of a 6.0 kg mass on Earth? (g = 10 N/kg)

commonErrors: Dividing mass by g instead of multiplying

questions.questionText: Explain two factors that would increase the braking distance of a car.

markScheme.answer: Higher speed increases braking distance because more kinetic energy must be transferred. Worn brakes reduce the braking force, meaning the car takes longer to stop.

steps.description: Identifies a valid factor, e.g. higher speed
Explains why: more kinetic energy needs to be transferred / greater distance to decelerate
Identifies a second valid factor, e.g. worn brakes, wet/icy road, worn tyres
Explains why: reduced friction / reduced braking force

commonErrors: Confusing braking distance with thinking distance
Naming factors that affect thinking distance (tiredness, alcohol) instead of braking distance

questions.questionText: A 1200 kg car is travelling at 15 m/s. Calculate the momentum of the car.

commonErrors: Incorrect unit — writing N instead of kg m/s

questions.questionText: A book rests on a table. Describe the Newton's third law pair for the weight of the book.

markScheme.answer: The Earth pulls the book downwards with a gravitational force. The book pulls the Earth upwards with an equal and opposite gravitational force.

steps.description: Identifies that the Earth exerts a gravitational pull on the book (weight)
Identifies that the book exerts an equal gravitational pull on the Earth
States the forces are equal in size, opposite in direction, and of the same type (gravitational)

commonErrors: Incorrectly pairing weight with the normal contact force from the table
Not specifying the type of force or the objects involved

questions.questionText: A crane lifts a 200 kg steel beam through a height of 15 m. Gravitational field strength g = 10 N/kg. Calculate the work done against gravity.

commonErrors: Using mass instead of weight in the calculation
